reviews.review_text: One of the best series of games I have ever played. It has a rich beautiful story and some of the best characters, Clementine and Lee. There was also this vibe when I was playing it, this feeling I can never get back when replaying it.

From the beginning with Lee and ending with clementine, the story, the journey getting there was magnificent.

10/10 would recomend
this game is so fun! my favorite is Lee, I can't wait for the boat arc with him and kenny in season 2!

Nice game, awesome storytelling, but sadly has some serious bugs with the save file system, for some people game progress does not gets saved or the save files doesn't load properly, there is a bunch of reddit threads and steam discussions on how to solve those issues, some solutions work for some people, some just don't. In my case the game is just not saving my progress at all. I tried a few solutions but none of them seem to work, and honestly no matter how good the game would actually be, I don't need to figure out how to solve a game bug when I paid money for it expecting it to work properly. If I would want to fix the game and make it run properly on my own before playing it, I would've downloaded a free pirate version instead of paying for it.

I have been a The Walking Dead fan for 9 years already.

I knew these games existed, but I never played them until I bought it on sale. I went in spoiler-free aside from the two major spoilers. I can not describe how it felt to experience this story for the very first time. 

Every single episode of this game is an experience of its own. From the day I bought it to the day I finished the main story, every time I turned my PC on, I wanted to do nothing but play these games.

As someone who has watched the show from beginning to end, I can confidently say that TWDG easily stands beside the first five seasons of The Walking Dead in terms of storytelling quality. If you loved the show, you will love Clementine. She will feel like your actual daughter. Every time someone hurts her, you will want to hurt them back. When she is happy, you are happy. When she is sad, you are sad.

One thing The Walking Dead never fails at is character writing, and Telltale absolutely nailed it here. Love or hate the characters, nearly every single one of them is written absurdly well.

I do not think I will ever bring myself to replaying it. This game is something everyone should experience at least once in their lifetime.

If you already love TWD, you will adore this game. If this is your first TWD experience, buckle up. Zombie stories don't get better than this.
A Timeless series even though it has it rough patches at times but when it hits it hits and it makes you wonder how telltale ever went under
I've played through this several times on Xbox, Ps4 and now PC. Love the story. It's a great game, highly recommended if you enjoy story driven choose your own adventure style games.
great story game. recomened to play at night. make sure you have about 1-2 hours for each episode
CINEMATIC MASTERPIECE BEST STORY IVE EVER EXPERIENCED, even tho not many survive throughout the story the game is still a banger
Ohhhh i love this game so much, i love lee and i like clementine, they're not same blood but, "they're still daughter and father"
Terrible game because almost all of my favorite characters died.
Played well over 100+ times over the years. Currently still trying to get the good ending.

I absolutely adore this game, before playing the walking dead, I wasn't all that fond of apocalyptic style games, but this series completely changed my mind on it! Clementine and Lee are some of the best protagonists in gaming, their relationship felt so organic and real, with season 1 having the most emotional ending I have ever seen in a game. The choices in this game also constantly come back and effect your later outcomes, really making you question if what you did was right along with being faced with a lot of moral dilemmas! The game play itself was also quite thrilling and easy to play if you're a new gamer! 
Every season of this game differs dramatically from the last, and out of the 4 seasons, every person who plays will probably have one they prefer over the other. Personally I didn't really enjoy season 3 because I felt like it ruined the climatic ending of season 2 by just revealing what happened in a bunch of flashbacks, while only getting to play as clementine half the time. However, some people I know prefer it the most because the choices in it have the biggest impacts! The 400 days DLC also felt really pointless as it had no effect on your gameplay in season 2 besides a few cutscenes. However, I do think the Michonne game is an under rated gem!
All in all playing this game, you're in for an emotional rollercoaster, and I could not recommend it enough!

What a great game i dont do reviews cuz i suck at them so yeah. 

but all the emotions this game put me through from S1-End was nuts the fact that you actually have to make hard decisions just adds something to the game that ive never seen before and it makes me feel so more immersed. i have no word other than incredible easy top 5 games of all time on my list.

Please play the game for yourself. You owe that to yourself
I wish TellTale was still a thing.

i was going to wait to play the Michonne before reviewing, but i am not getting into it enough to cruise through it.  I am going to review the rest of it.  i bought this game back in 2022, played it very little, didnt like how it handled and never touched it again till recently, 2026.  the 1st season is kind of rough.  i didnt like how it handled and the space you have to move around is awkward as well.  the way you select things feels a little off also.  they do improve on the handling and how it moves as the game goes on, from season to season.  so if thats what your hang up is try to keep pushing through the series.  one annoying thing for me was they kept changing what buttons do each season.  as a person that mainly uses controllers but was using a keyboard for this that kept throwing me off each season. i died more times then i should have due to this.   however the story is very good.  after not giving it a chance the 1st go around im glad i finally came back to it.  the story is just that good.  you make some big decisions that affects who will be with you at the time and in the future.  from the sound of things each season will end fairly similar regardless what you do but the middle can be very different each time.  it shows you multiple ways things can end up at the end of each episode which shows it can vary.  The character Clementine will probably be my most memorable character i have ever played.  ive been playing games for over 20 years.  they just do a great job making you care about her.  if you can get past the sort of clunky handling and want a good story with characters you can care about.  i highly recommend this.

Story is sad yeah, I mean it is the walking dead so one shouldn't expect something happy. The game itself is decent I only wish there was a skip or fast forward for some of the conversations. Would def recommend giving it a try.
Great game, the story is amazing and emotional. This game has made me cry many times. 10/10 would recomend!
One of the best story games ever made and definitely a must play if you're a fan of the decision making genre. The definitive edition usually goes on sale pretty often so absolutely worth the money!
maybe biased to the fact I'm a big fan of all things Walking dead anyway but also a fan of Telltale and the whole style and game play as well.
S1 carried this game for the most part. S2 and S4 reached that intensity for a little while here and there.
It’s so damn good. Loved the gameplay, the characters, the world, and the story. Please buy this game if you haven't already.

The Walking Dead: Telltale is the best Telltale has ever been. It has tension, lovable characters, an amazing main character in Clementine, and it has several pivotal choices. The stakes felt high, the tension was thick, and my tears flowed. This is an emotional ride of a story.

10/10
Havent finshed the game because my save file got lost idk and stopped playing.
10/10 except the new frontier SUCKS and the final season has bugged settings that make your game crash, be wary

На мой взгляд, сюжет этого кинца, лучше чем в сериале, перонажи становятся как родные, за них переживаешь, если вы не безчувственный психопат, многое даже до слёз довести может. Но прежде чем играть, задумайтесь! Все истории заканчиваются клиф хенгерами (с заделом на продолжение), студия закрыта, продолжение вы уже никогда не увидите...

Хоть истории закончены и рассказаны, разрабы не решились поставить жирную точку не в истории Мишон, не в итории Клементины и это чувство пустоты и жедание увидить продолжение, меня покинет не скоро...

Oh my god this game was so good so good that I cried after the last season the music the game (I have never cried for any game before) everything was perfection. I felt so connected to everyone idk how they did that so well. I have played games where they try this but not of them even compare to this one. I loved how interactive it was and it felt like I was there. You have to have fast reflects or you it may not go your way. The amount of decisions where there wasn't really a right answer was a lot and it was very hard to choose. I sometimes had to pause to decide what I really wanted to do. Overall this game is a 10/10, you can play it again but I'm not I haven't played any of the bonuses so I can't comment on those.
Edit: I played the bonus's and Michonne and I like the bonus in season 1 but Michonne I thought was kinda mid it's more of a realistic story tho with here trauma living with here through the whole story but I didn't really feel a very strong connection with anyone in Michonne. I felt bad for Michonne but it wasn't cry worthy or anything. My honest opinion you can play game with out Michonne. I do recommend playing the 400 days bonus I didn't in my playthrough but going back to it I wish I did because there's some backstory to characters that will come in the future.

DON'T BUY GAMES INDIVIDUALLY buy this when its on sale as its the best price and fixed bugs glitches and more stuff, I use this lightly that was "broken" in other versions and this bundle includes the dlc which is Kool, but not worth buying on its own as it was a let down of a dlc and the Michonne game is overly hated but its really good and worth it, these games are all good I personally don't like that the 3rd game I don't like that Clem isn't the main character and its Javier but excluding Clem from the game would make no sense and hence the CLEMINTINE PARADOX look it up on YouTube if you don't know what I'm talking about, its a really interesting topic.

One of my most favorite story games, it is so immersive to my favorite show and the world around it. I love TWD show and comics and I love having another outlet to the franchise and I never get tired of replaying it
One of the greatest gaming experience ever, you feel connected to the characters, some of the decision are literally hard to decide, Clementine is the GOAT

One of my favorite game series I've ever played. Season One is my personal favorite. This game is perfect if you love the Walking Dead as a franchise, but also want a story that is very character driven, with heartbreak, emotional attachments, and a truly beautiful story between a man, his past, and the uncertain future of raising a child in a world of the undead. The other seasons are good too, and follow a similar formula. Even the least popular season of the series has it's own personal perks, to where I can't even say any of the seasons are bad at all. If you want to play a game series in its entirety that focuses on a story you have small parts in changing, this game series is for you.
the BEST choices matter game to exist,but only downside is telltale studio went down the drain after this and I was really excited for TWAU2 but it won't be releasing for at least 2-3 years

Some issues like saves files not saving to the cloud and some basic mechanical issues on PC like issues with selecting a screen to display the game, but overall the story is great so its worth it. The mechanics can be a little slow but you can really feel the improvement on that part when it comes to the later games like episode 4 which had a great graphical upgrade and some chances to mechanics to make it feel a little more snappy. 

overall the story is great, devastating and depressing at times (though it can be a little slow in earlier seasons) to the point I struggled to finish, but I personally found the conclusion to be worth the playthrough. Overall it was a good game, worth the playthrough and price. Would recommend.

Game is incredible, but, I would have loved if the story was ACTUALLY tailored from your options. Some things in the game would have been so much better if you could prevent that event from happening. Overall great game, but misleading.

First game is quite good. If you play it only once. 

Sequels went the same way as TWD show and became very predictable melodramas

Me ha gustado muchísimo el juego. Es bien interesante la modalidad de tomar decisiones. A veces pienso que se parece a la vida en sí misma. Personalmente, me ayuda mucho a reflexionar el juego acerca de las decisiones que tomamos en la vida.
ENG: I like this game a lot. It's very interesting the modality of take decisions. Sometimes I think that it's very similar to the real life. Personally, the game's help to think about the decision that we take in our lives.

Best Telltale game/series
Best choices matter story driven game out there

This game is genuinely amazing, the story elements for a zombie apocalypse situation are great, the characters are written realistically (for the most part), but it's a telltale game, you don't control ♥♥♥♥

One of the best story games I have ever played!, Watching youtuber's play this when growing up always made me want to play this, Now that I have started playing this game, its one of the best! 10/10 would recommend!
amazing game.  the story, atmosphere, the visuals just amazing!!

Some of the best stories in story games are here. Hugely impressive with the whole 'choose your own adventure' storytelling does on the immersiveness of the player. That being said, when given gameplay, it is VERY terrible. Particularly in s4

A fun game worth experiencing. Had some issues with bugs and getting the game to run. If you are getting the infinite loading screen issue, removing the save file helped fixed the issue for me. The Definitive Edition package could have been better but still worth experiencing the entire series.

By the end of the main series, its like being hit by a truck. And when you think you're just about done, it shifts to reverse and runs you over again.

10/10

Absolutely gorgeous and beautiful game, full of well-written characters and the story is god tier; I wish I had the time to explore every aspect of it and see every choice you can make, but it's alright, I played as I could and I'm feeling quite happy. I will miss this beautiful game and I'm glad it ended without useless 5th or 6th seasons. And I have to talk about the OSTs, which are so damn scary and beautiful and iconic. And lastly, they really nailed the atmosphere and each time I hit play I feel like I enter another world.. that's how good this game is. Goodbye, Clem.

∞/10!! this is a masterpiece of a game! it is easily one of the best games I have ever played. all the series from 1 to 4 even the extra game was peak! the best one is obviously the first one then the fourth. I was shocked of how much the gameplay improved! Must play for super rich choice-based games lovers.
These games are like books to me. Telltale games had to go bankrupt ofc as everything good in our world never lasts
Amazing game but whichever ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ game developer decided that kenny has to die in season3 is evil and i hope the rest of ur life sucks and is full of bad luck and misery
Brilliant writing and development. We all wish the story could continue.

Rewelacyjna gra!!! Najlepszy tytuł interaktywny z wyborami w jaki grałem, świetnie oddaje klimat seriali TWD no i ta gra, to jest po prostu zaj*bisty serial XD Cudna fabułka, postacie, pełno decyzji, malutkie błędy ale znikają za mgłą piękna tej produkcji. Dużo pozytywnych emocji zostawiłem w tej gierce. Bardzo polecam każdemu!!!

the only good game is the first,afterwards the dialogues make what could be good stories unbarrable. forced decisions on you, "your choices matter" yeah sure buddy. no surprise the company went broke. also,only 16,6 % of people managed to finish season 4,it's not hard gameplay,just not worth your time. finished the last season,oh so praised,did a few times alt f4 to see different dialogues and choices. the "choice matters" tag should be taken off. wdy mean either Justin dies or AJ becomes a psycho?
Awesome having all TWD content in one application. Amazing storytelling!
super fun never watched the series but i enjoyed this immensely good for fans of life is strange
10/10 ... The Best Game to teach teenagers that Actions have consequences!
one of the choices matter goats for sure, 10 outta 10 for me

THIS GAME IS SO AMAZING!!! I have such maternal instincts for clementine. ngl tho she always gets in the worst groups EVER!! somehow an 11 year old was more capable of every adult in her group COMBINED in season 2. most adults in this game are so incompetent. anyways, I like how your choices actually matter in how people view you. I also love how every character has their flaws, especially kenny. kenny is the GOAT but we also get to see so many sides from him which is really realistic imo. if you like choice based games ur gonna love this TRUST!!!!
Very lucky to get this for a really good price on sale. Great stuff, you can do all 4 seasons + the 3 episodes of Michonne, I'm about to finish it and will start 400 Days right after.
i remember watching gameplays of the first and second season in youtube cause i didn't have a pc back then and now being able to finish it really made me happy, i will keep it forever in my heart.

The Walking Dead: Telltale Definitive Series is a good collection of games that tells Clementine’s story from childhood at the start of the outbreak, through the years that pass, as she grows up so fast in this harsh world and takes care of AJ post-Season 2. Each season builds and builds Clem's character, mirroring what Lee did in Season 1, as both Season 1 and Season 4 complement each other. While Seasons 2 and 3 feel somewhat unfocused, they are my favourites, as they tackle different themes and ideas uniquely and interestingly, though they lead to some plot holes. I like all of the Seasons as a whole, as it feels like a natural conclusion for Clem and AJ, knowing that Season 2 was meant to be darker, and a different ending puts it in perspective that if Walking Dead ended in Season 2, the story would be different, and so would be Clem’s and AJ’s. This whole series is a masterpiece in storytelling, and I wish a played it sooner, as this is my first ever time experiencing this Masterpiece. I would highly recommend playing it, as it is easy to play but tests your morals and your character on how to raise a child during an outbreak.
 
Clementine will remember that.

Season 1 and 2 are some of the most pinnacle games in their genre. Helped define choice based story games throughout the early and mid 2010's. Its some of Telltales best work ever

A New Frontier is also heavily underrated with a great new MC. And The Final Season is the cherry on top to a wonderful franchise.

What can I even say about this series that hasn't already been said? It has it's high ups, and low downs, but regardless, a story worth of your time.

Playing this series, start to finish, is a dream come true. I remember being so little, seeing myself in Clementine's shoes, she "grew" along with me, and I believe that to be something magic. I remember watching closely as each episode was launched, each season, each DLC. I saw so many playthroughs, searching for the alternative endings, fun facts, deep dives, I was obsessed with it. I played the series on Xbox, but got soft locked with a bug, I was so sad about it, to have to play a chapter that wasn't built on my decisions. Acquiring this series on my computer was a must for me.

I see the first season as a true master piece, even though it had the same problems the TV show has, too many irrelevant characters only to have them die in instants. It might be my nostalgia, but I think that the characters that do have any relevance are well explored and treated, you're given a chance to know them, to care for them. The story is entertaining, immersive, you feel what Lee feels, and I think that a big part of that was because of his relationship with Clementine. Emotion is what ties the user to the story, and the first season is full of that. The ending feels real, too real, makes you hopeful and fearful for Clementine.

To me, the second season is one of the WORST. Too many characters, that for some reason REFUSE to treat Clem the way she deserves! You can not convince me that's how adults would treat a child in her situation. The relationships you make don't have the same impact as the one in the first season, something just feels off, like we didn't get enough time with any of the characters. I did like Kenny's arc, could've been better, he deserved better, but a melancholic ending fits him just fine (in my playthrough, obviously). Although we didn't get as much time with Jane, I kind of liked her character, sadly, they never gave her the importance she needed to truly become remarkable, or even give some deeper understanding of her. I do think a melancholic ending fits Jane, I just wish they have done it in a different way.

The third season, A New Frontier, is alright. It does have the "too many characters" problem, but (at least with the main family), the relationships are somewhat explored, I just wanted more time to care for Javier and the others. Some characters in this season also feel too...blank, to say the least, falling into stereotypes and not being developed at all. I was also disappointed with the fact that this season wasn't really that open with your romance options, why bother to give us the choice if we'll be forced to follow a single narrative?

Finally, the final season, man what a BLAST! I haven't felt this way since my first time watching the first season. It's a HUGE upgrade of the gameplay style, I loved what they did with it. The collectables were a nice idea. The story was extremely immersive, I've found myself TERRIFIED in some occasions. I cried many times, especially in the last episodes. This cycle created by Lee was treated so well, I cared for AJ the same way I cared for Clementine. Although there was no time to explore all the characters, I feel like each of them got a bit of time to shine (except Omar, my poor Omar), it never felt overwhelming. The finale makes me speechless, it handles the cycle Lee created so well. I loved every single bit of it.

I'll refrain from talking about Michonne's DLC, since I feel like I would be getting into a review of the show, and that's a whole other thing... But I mean- you get the entire series AND a DLC? It won't ever be a bad deal hehe!
unoptimized and crashes constantly
The best Telltale has ever made, so far the peak of choice based games.

I dont see much of a difference to original series , though this time im playing on Xbox controller , and sometimes there's a bug showing multiple options under same button ( example , at drug store , i had a choice of using axe and keys on the door on same button - which is a bug , it should be like it.

So far so good. 

Loved the original series , finished them all , gonna see what's different here.

Definetly a steal when on sale :D
"haha clem you fool! you killed the wrong person! I was the ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ all along with no warnings whatsoever, you ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ moron"

An Absolute Masterpiece – Incredible Value! (9.5/10)

I just wrapped up the entire 55-hour journey, and wow... few games have ever left a lasting mark on me like this one. Getting this whole collection on a discount is hands down one of the best gaming purchases I've ever made.

Here is my quick breakdown of the pros and cons:

PROS:

The Story: Watching Clementine grow from a vulnerable kid into a fierce survivor is one of the greatest character arcs in gaming history. The emotional weight of your choices is incredible, and the writing genuinely made me care about the characters.

The Graphics: The upgraded "Graphic Black" comic-book art style looks gorgeous. It makes all the older seasons feel cohesive, polished, and beautifully atmospheric.

The Gameplay: Smooth, immersive, and responsive. It balances quiet, tense dialogue with frantic, heart-pounding action perfectly.

Insane Value: You get four full seasons, DLCs, and bonus content. Getting 55 hours of top-tier storytelling for a discounted price is an absolute steal.

CONS:

The Illusion of Choice: At times, you realize that major plot points and specific character fates are set in stone, no matter what choices you make.

Minor Bugs: You will still hit occasional legacy bugs like brief audio drops or clunky camera transitions, but nothing game-breaking.

Season 3 Pacing: When playing back-to-back, Season 3 feels a little detached from the core emotional weight of the rest of the series.

Final Verdict:
If you want an unforgettable, story-driven experience that will completely suck you in, buy this immediately. Especially if it's on sale!
The original games had Steam Cloud support. Why not the DEFINITIVE version?
shouldve made kenny an unkillable maniac 0 remorse cold blooded dude and brought him back in the final season, since i decided not to (you know) make the kenny choice in the ending of season 2
its very enjoyable if you like a casual narrative story telling game
Nice game so far, will update when I am further along
LEE Y CLEMENTINE LOS MEJORES PERSONAJES DE LA HISTORIA, como lloré con este juego...
Bro. Why would Tell Tale do that to me? Fantastic story from start to end.
Forgot to write the review........Great game .......Cried a lot ....Recommended

Officially completed my replay of the entire series, and I enjoyed it just as much as I did the first time. I've watched a few let's plays as well, I couldn't recommend this series more. From the main titles, to the DLC, this game is filled with lively characters, stressful/silly/emotional moments, action-packed sequences, along with a "choose your own adventure" kind of feeling.

Games with a zombie apocalypse setting always seem to draw me in, but TWD has been an enjoyable world for me to invest in for years now. If you've got the time and money, definitely snag yourself a copy and go through the adventure yourself!

This is the first zombie apocalypse game I ever played and I played it while watching TWD for the first time. I played a metric ton of these games and yes, they do have their flaws, but I still wanted to replay this until I had seen EVERY outcome. I never cared for the 'your choices don't actually matter' thing. Love TWD, and love that I got to play a decision-based game in one of my favourite IPs ever

The Walking Dead: The Telltale Definitive Series is easily one of the most emotionally powerful gaming experiences I’ve ever played. This is not just a game, it’s a story that hits you right in the gut over and over again.

The choices you make actually matter in the most painful and beautiful ways. There were so many moments where I just sat there staring at the screen, knowing whatever I picked was going to hurt someone no matter what. That level of emotional weight is something very few games ever achieve.

The art style is simple but incredibly effective, and it somehow makes every scene hit even harder. Combined with the voice acting and storytelling, it pulls you completely into Clementine’s journey from start to finish.

It’s heartbreaking, intense, and unforgettable. One of the best narrative-driven games ever made, no question.

This game got me in my feels and I'm not usually the emotional type. The storytelling, the characters, the choices with no clear right answer, it genuinely made me question my moral compass.

Only gripe is some deaths feel kinda ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥, like how are people dying to these slow ass walkers lol.

Overall tho, easily one of the best story games I've played.

I enjoyed playing this game. The story is really amazing.
I can say that Lee and Clem are the best duo in the history of all games. Watching Clem transform from a little, scared girl into an adult, helping little AJ in the way she learned from Lee is really amazing.
I got into the game max. I wouldn't mind if this story continued. I'll be waiting for that day with impatience.

I've always seen people play this game, but never sat and watched it for too long until I decide to purchase it and I must say that these has been the most joyful video-game playthrough I've ever done I loved the unique sound track this game has, and the episode nature of this games creates an pit inside once you finish another and I must say I didn't want to finish this game since I didn't want to end but it's definitely up there among my favorite the only con I have about this game is the bad path finding in the first season but besides that there's not much to say. I LOVE THIS GAME

season1 is gold, 2 is a fan favourite though I think its a bit of a mess, I adore season 3 and 4 is a fantastic end, Sometimes the choices feel a little limited, a flaw in which telltale style games likely suffer but other than a few decisions being annoying or not resulting in what you think, A fantastic game, especially if you can pick it up on discount
Great game, close to the serie. Good sound effects and audio, and the graphics grew on me...it really adds to the experience
